#include "class.h"
#include "constantpool.h"
#include "jstring.h"
#include "memory.h"
#include "utf8_string.h"

Detailed Description

Constant pool functions implementation

Definition in file constantpool.c.

Function Documentation

const_pool_t* cp_create ( class_t *  cl,
class_file_t *  cf 
)

Creates a new constant-pool from the specified class-file

Parameters:
cl A pointer to the class owning the constant pool
cf A pointer to an open class-file
Returns:
A pointer to the newly created constant-pool

Creates a dummy, empty constant pool used by array classes

Returns:
A pointer to the dummy constant pool

char* cp_get_class_name ( const_pool_t *  cp,
uint16_t  entry 
)

Gets the the name of a class, fails if the entry parameter is out of range or if the relevant entry has a tag different from CONSTANT_Class

Parameters:
cp A pointer to a valid constant pool object
entry The constant pool entry number
Returns:
The index of the class name contained in the specified entry

void cp_set_tag_and_data ( const_pool_t *  cp,
uint16_t  entry,
uint8_t  tag,
void *  data 
)

Sets the tag and data of an element of the constant pool, the data is a generic pointer, this function is used for replacing index-based reference with direct pointers after resolution

Parameters:
cp A pointer to a valid constant pool object
entry The constant pool entry number
tag The new tag
data The new data
